scenario: Iris Healthcare runs the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot Starter on 4 application servers and uses OIDC tokens to authenticate to a SaaS cluster handling 9000 records per day. The platform team must confirm the documented default of camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path so it knows where access tokens are cached on disk by default before locking down the file system.
question: What is the default value of camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot Starter properties reference?

options.text: The default value is $HOME/.camunda/credentials, the path to the credentials cache file used when authenticating the Camunda client to the OAuth provider
The default value is $HOME/.zeebe/credentials, the path to the credentials cache file used when authenticating the Camunda client to the OAuth provider
The default value is /tmp/camunda-credentials, the path to the credentials cache file used when authenticating the Camunda client to the OAuth provider
The default value is null, so the client never caches tokens and re-requests a fresh access token from the OAuth provider on every API call to Camunda

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.auth section lists camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path with a default value of $HOME/.camunda/credentials. The description identifies this property as the path to the credentials cache file used by the OAuth authentication flow.

b.text: Incorrect. The legacy Zeebe client path under $HOME/.zeebe/credentials is not the documented Camunda 8.8 default; the new starter defaults to $HOME/.camunda/credentials.

c.text: Incorrect. /tmp/camunda-credentials is not the documented default; the reference shows $HOME/.camunda/credentials inside the user home directory.

d.text: Incorrect. The property has a concrete default ($HOME/.camunda/credentials), so the client does cache credentials by default rather than re-requesting tokens on every call.

explanation: The Spring Boot Starter properties reference shows camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path with a default of $HOME/.camunda/credentials. That file holds the cached OAuth credentials used when authenticating the Camunda client, and operators can override the path to relocate or harden the cache.
